game_name: Advanced Dungeons & Dragons: Pool of Radiance

design_philosophy.core_loop: Create a party of AD&D characters, explore first-person dungeons, engage in tactical turn-based combat, gain experience to level up abilities, progress through story beats toward confronting the evil leader
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (HP, spell slots, positioning) during turn-based tactical combat against escalating enemy encounters; character death prevention through tactical healing and positioning
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a custom adventuring party through a Forgotten Realms dungeon crawl, making meaningful character build decisions (race/class/alignment), discovering loot, and overcoming organized evil forces through tactical superiority
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict adherence to AD&D 1st/2nd Edition ruleset for character mechanics; immediate turn resolution in combat (not wait-for-all-commands); alignment system affects NPC interactions; multi-classing trade-offs (slower leveling for versatility); class-specific tactical abilities (fighter sweeps, thief backstabs, magic-user spells) create asymmetric party roles

difficulty.lives_system: Party-based with per-character death mechanic: characters reach 0 HP and require bandaging by another party member to prevent permanent death; up to 6 party members with 2 NPC slots provides redundancy
difficulty.continues_system: Save-game system allows unlimited reloading from named save files; no checkpoint system mentioned
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players design their own party compositions with race/class/alignment choices; can redistribute generated ability scores; optional computer AI can handle combat decisions for accessibility; different class abilities create variable tactical approaches

progression.upgrade_system: Experience points earned from combat encounters allow characters to gain levels, increasing capabilities per AD&D rules; magic-users and clerics memorize spells as they level; fighters gain multiple attacks per round and sweep abilities at higher levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated: party enlisted to clear areas around port city of Phlan, progressing from one area to another, building toward confronting the powerful evil leader; NPC recruitment through dialogue/parley
replayability_hooks: Character export to subsequent Gold Box games preserves progression
Multiple race/class/alignment combinations create different party experiences
Ability score customization allows min-maxing experiments
Multi-class options for non-humans provide alternative power curves

world_building_techniques: Setting within Forgotten Realms canonical world (Phlan port city)
Monster depictions drawn from AD&D Monster Manual for lore consistency
Character alignment system affecting NPC reactions
Quest structure: clear marauding inhabitants, confront evil leader

boss_design.philosophy: Encounters follow standard AD&D encounter tables; progression toward a final confrontation with the powerful leader of evil forces; random encounters use three sprite scales (short/medium/long-range) to vary perceived threat distance
notable_bosses: The powerful leader of the evil forces (final confrontation, not named in source)

faction_indicators: Alignment system (moral philosophy choices) affecting NPC reactions
Character class roles create party identity (fighter/cleric/mage/thief archetypes)
danger_signals: Character death risk creates tension in party management
environmental_storytelling: Graveyard explorable area suggests undead threats
Port city of Phlan setting implies trade conflicts
Enemy occupation of surrounding areas implies organized threat
First-person exploration perspective creates dungeon immersion
